answers.field_label: If Print Job Is Ongoing Please Provide The Time Frame
answers.value: This is an ongoing monthly print and mail job scheduled to run on the second Tuesday of each month. Each cycle includes printing, inserting, and mailing approximately 18,000–22,000 confidential notices related to birth record corrections, adoption record updates, and amendments to vital records. The anticipated duration is three years, aligned with the current contract term (July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2028), with an option to renew if the program is extended. Volume may increase by up to 10–15% annually based on historical trends and projected population growth in Marion, Lake, Allen, and Hamilton counties. Turnaround from data file submission to mail induction is expected to be no more than five business days each month, with emergency off-cycle runs (no more than two per year) for statutory or court-ordered notifications.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: This job involves printing personalized letters that include limited confidential information derived from the Indiana Birth Registration System (IBRS), including partial Social Security numbers (last four digits only), full legal names of parents and children, dates of birth, and internal case reference numbers. Each letter is matched to a single addressee and is mailed in a double-window security envelope with no sensitive information visible through the window other than recipient name and mailing address. All content is generated from encrypted data files transmitted via the State-approved secure file transfer protocol (SFTP).
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: The contractor must ensure that all print files and mail pieces are handled in a secure, access-controlled production environment. Workstations used for this job must automatically lock after 10 minutes of inactivity, and all printed overruns, make-ready sheets, test prints, and misprints must be immediately segregated into locked shred bins clearly labeled "CONFIDENTIAL – VITAL RECORDS". No samples may be retained for marketing or training purposes. Upon completion of each monthly run, the contractor will provide a destruction log indicating the total number of spoiled or excess pieces destroyed, the method of destruction (cross-cut shredding or secure pulping), the date and time of destruction, and the name or initials of the supervising operator who witnessed the process.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: Any suspected or confirmed unauthorized disclosure (for example, envelopes returned due to misdirected address, multiple letters inserted into a single envelope, or visible confidential information through the envelope window) must be reported to the Indiana Department of Health Contract Manager and the IDOA Contract Representative within one business day of discovery. The incident report should include a description of the issue, approximate number of affected records, root cause analysis if known, and immediate corrective actions taken. Future process improvements (such as additional camera-matching verification during insertion, barcoded integrity checks, or enhanced address validation) should be documented and proposed in writing within 10 business days of the incident to prevent recurrence.
